DISCOVERY 302, PETITIONER CHELMSFORD BETTER NOT BIGGER, ROLAND VAN LIEW'S PETITION FOR ORDER ENFORCING SUBPOENA AND AWARDING SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED FROM MAY 7, 2015 TO MAY 18, 2015 AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302. JUDGE PRO TEM: PHILIP ANDERSON. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS CHELMSFORD BETTER NOT BIGGER, AND ROLAND VAN LIEW'S PETITION FOR ORDER ENFORCING SUBPOENA AND AWARDING SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. AS TO THE MEET AND CONFER ISSUE, THE COURT FINDS THERE WAS SUBSTANTIAL CORRESPONDENCE BY E-MAIL, AND THE LACK OF A MEETING IS NOT A BASIS FOR QUASHING THE SUBPOENA. AS TO THE FIRST AMENDMENT ISSUE AND WHETHER OR NOT THE CONTENT OF THE TWITTER ACCOUNT IS DEFAMATORY, THE COURT FINDS THE CONTENT TO BE DEFAMATORY ON ITS FACE, AND CONTAINS NO NON-DEFAMATORY CONTENT. THE COURT AWARDS PETITIONERS SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO STATUTE IN THE AMOUNT OF $12,500.00 TO BE PAID BY TWITTER, INC. TWITTER, INC. MUST COMPLY WITH PRODUCTION REGARDING THE SUBPOENA TO PETITIONERS BY NO LATER THAN 5/22/15. PETITIONERS COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT CONTAINING THE FACTS AND AUTHORITY THE COURT RELIED ON IN ITS RULING BASED ON THE PLEADINGS AND ORAL ARGUMENT PRESENTED TO THE COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: JOANNA BROADWELL, CSR #10959 (TEL NO. 415-274-9977) (302/EHG)

Non-Party Twitter, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Production Order
Off Calendar Jun-17-2015 Off Calendar

Non-Party Twitter, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Production Order
Off Calendar Jun-18-2015 Continued To Jun-29-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Petition For Order Enforcing Subpoena And Awarding Sanctions
Off Calendar May-18-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ERIC J. DILULIO of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP, Tel No. (415) 434-9100 appearing for Petitioners Chelmsford Better Not Bigger, and Roland Van Liew;
Atty. JULIE E. SCHWARTZ of Perkins Coie LLP, Tel No. (650) 838-4300 appearing for Non-Party Twitter, Inc.

RULING - The Court required parties to appear at hearing. Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court ruled as follows: PETITIONERS CHELMSFORD BETTER NOT BIGGER, AND ROLAND VAN LIEW'S Petition For Order Enforcing Subpoena And Awarding Sanctions is granted. As to the meet and confer issue, the Court finds there was substantial correspondence by e-mail, and the lack of a meeting is not a basis for quashing the subpoena. As to the First Amendment issue and whether or not the content of the Twitter account is defamatory, the Court finds the content to be defamatory on its face, and contains no non-defamatory content. The Court awards Petitioners sanctions pursuant to statute in the amount of $12,500.00 to be paid by Twitter, Inc. Twitter, Inc. must comply with production regarding the subpoena to Petitioners by no later than 5/22/15. PETITIONERS COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT CONTAINING THE FACTS AND AUTHORITY THE COURT RELIED ON IN ITS RULING BASED ON THE PLEADINGS AND ORAL ARGUMENT PRESENTED TO THE COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Joanna Broadwell, CSR #10959 (Tel No. 415-274-9977) (302/EHG)

Notice Of Petition For Order Enforcing Subpoena And Awarding Sanctions
Off Calendar May-07-2015 Continued To May-18-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Danielle Kennedy of Sheppard Mullin telephone number 415-774-3225 for Plaintiff.
Julie Schwartz of Perkins Coie telephone number 650-838-4490 for Twitter, Inc. (Non-Party).

Counsel for the Plaintiff did not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem. The matter was argued. The Court did not adopt the tentative ruling. PETITIONER CHELMSFORD BETTER NOT BIGGER, ROLAND VAN LIEW'S Petition For Order Enforcing Subpoena And Awarding Sanctions is continued to May 18, 2015 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Anderson. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)
